Whitelist & Blacklist

---

## Whitelist Approach: Accept Only Specific Values

A whitelist defines exactly which values are acceptable. Continue the loop if input does NOT match any whitelisted value.

```cpp
string response;
cout << "Continue? (yes/no): ";
cin >> response;

while (response != "yes" && response != "no") {
    cout << "Invalid. Enter yes or no: ";
    cin >> response;
}
```

<!-- Slide 3 -->

---

## Blacklist Approach: Reject Specific Bad Values

A blacklist defines which values are unacceptable. The loop continues as long as the input matches a forbidden value.

```cpp
string username;
cout << "Enter a username (cannot be 'admin'): ";
cin >> username;

while (username == "admin") {
    cout << "That username is reserved. Try again: ";
    cin >> username;
}
```


<!-- Slide 4 -->

---
